commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=ae5d763f9807020c27ab789b207c0719d3d908c1)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes reply-session initialization to compare persisted `skillsSnapshot` shape and awaits inbound session metadata recording before route updates.

PR surface: Source +20. Total +20 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. The linked issue gives a concrete two-message Discord path, and current source shows persisted `promptRef` and hydrated `prompt` shapes can differ under the whole-entry JSON revision guard; I did not run live Discord in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor revision and artifact-backed real behavior proof; this is not safe to route as an automated repair because the external Discord proof gate remains contributor-side.

target.title: fix(sessions): align reply init revision with persisted skillsSnapshot
target.body:
## Summary

- Compare reply session initialization revisions using the on-disk `skillsSnapshot` shape (`promptRef`) instead of hydrated writer-cache `prompt` text
- Await inbound session meta recording before `updateLastRoute` to avoid concurrent session store writes

Fixes #96698

## Problem

The second inbound Discord message (and likely any channel) fails with:

```
reply session initialization conflicted for agent:main:discord:channel:<channelId>
```

`createReplySessionInitializationRevision()` serializes the full session entry, but snapshot load and commit compared different `skillsSnapshot` shapes: persisted `{ skills, promptRef }` vs hydrated `{ skills, prompt }`. This produced false `stale-snapshot` conflicts after the first agent run.

## Test plan

- [x] Send two consecutive messages in the same Discord channel; both succeed
- [x] No `stale-snapshot` / initialization conflict on second turn
- [ ] Existing session store tests pass

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
target.author: wiidz

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
